gap_topic: Cross-JPY pairs (EUR/JPY, GBP/JPY, AUD/JPY, NZD/JPY) appear in the PM instrument-universe but are NOT in _JPY_CORRELATED frozenset at exposure_aggregator.py:46. If either paper loop ever generates a signal on a cross-JPY pair, the aggregator will treat that position's notional as NON-JPY-correlated — under-counting JPY tail risk. Not blocking for current USDJPY-only scope.
suggested_resolution: Confirm that for paper Phase 1 the only traded pair is USDJPY (no cross-JPY signals fire), OR amend the frozenset before enabling cross-JPY trading.

gap_topic: No verification was performed of WHETHER cross-host or symlink-based deployment topologies could defeat the fcntl advisory lock. fcntl.flock provides mutual exclusion only between processes on the same kernel sharing the same file. If deployment evolves to two-host topology (e.g., paper loops running in containers with separate filesystems), the lock becomes vacuous. Current topology (single workstation) is assumed and binding; any deployment change is a CRO re-review trigger.
suggested_resolution: Document single-host constraint in deployment runbook; add CRO re-review trigger for any container/multi-host deployment change.

gap_topic: NHT did not independently execute the gate suite (pytest/ruff/forbidden-phrases) to re-verify Wave-10 closures — relied on Wave-10 NHT re-verification artifact records. If the artifact text drifts from current code state, NHT Claim 3 verdict is conditional on the artifact's accuracy.
suggested_resolution: At next gate, NHT or QD should re-execute the full test suite to verify current code state matches Wave-10 artifact records.

gap_topic: Whether the firm has explicitly adopted the PM-crystallized CF-T9 disclosure wording as binding policy (as opposed to a sample wording satisfying the NHT requirement) is not stated in any artifact NHT read. This affects the interpretation of SD-6 done_when criterion strictness.
suggested_resolution: CEO or PM should explicitly record at ratification time whether the PM-crystallized clause is adopted as binding firm policy wording, or whether substantive conformance is the operative test.

gap_topic: The redacted CRO artifact shows seven BC-8-LIFT-COND-* labels, but only BC-8-LIFT-COND-7 survives label redaction. PR cannot independently verify which three of the seven PM enumerated inline in SD-2 done_when. Resolving F-002 requires PM to either name all seven by ID or explicitly incorporate them by reference.
suggested_resolution: CONSENSUS SD-2 body must either enumerate all seven BC-8-LIFT-COND-* IDs explicitly or contain a by-reference clause adopting all seven from the CRO artifact.

gap_topic: Wave-10 binding wording on BC-8 (CEO ratification 2026-05-06) does not enumerate the seven CRO BC-8-LIFT-COND constraints. Whether those constraints were considered ratified on 2026-05-06 along with the lift, or are a separate Wave-2 emission specific to 2026-05-10 paper-launch, is a lineage question PM must resolve in SD-2 body.
suggested_resolution: PM should clarify in CONSENSUS whether the seven CRO constraints (emitted 2026-05-10) are additive to the 2026-05-06 ratification or are the complete operative set for paper-launch authorization.
